curriculum.title: HTML, CSS and JavaScript Fundamentals
curriculum.description: Learn how to build webpages, style them with colors and layouts, and make them interactive using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

lessons.title: What is HTML?
lessons.body: HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It is used to create the structure of a webpage.
lessons.avatar_script: Think of HTML like the skeleton of a webpage. It tells the browser where headings, paragraphs, and images should go.

questions.question: What is HTML used for?
questions.explanation: HTML builds the structure of webpages.

questions.question: HTML creates the structure of a webpage.
questions.explanation: HTML organizes content like headings and paragraphs.

questions.question: Create a heading that says Hello.
questions.explanation: Use the h1 tag for main headings. Write an opening tag, your text, then a closing tag with a forward slash.

code_example.explanation: Use the h1 tag to create a heading by writing an opening angle bracket, then h1, close the bracket, write Hello, then close the tag with slash h1.

lessons.title: What is CSS?
lessons.body: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is used to make webpages colorful and visually appealing.
lessons.avatar_script: If HTML builds the house, CSS decorates it. CSS adds colors, spacing, and styles.

questions.question: What does CSS do?
questions.explanation: CSS controls how webpages look.

questions.question: CSS changes how a webpage looks.
questions.explanation: CSS adds visual style.

questions.question: Change paragraph text color to red.
questions.explanation: Target the p element with a CSS rule and use the color property to set the text color value.

code_example.explanation: Write p followed by curly brackets. Inside write color colon red semicolon.

lessons.title: What is JavaScript?
lessons.avatar_script: JavaScript makes webpages interactive. It can respond to clicks, show messages, and update content.

questions.question: What does JavaScript do?
questions.explanation: JavaScript makes webpages interactive.

questions.question: JavaScript can show messages on a webpage.
questions.explanation: JavaScript can create alerts and responses.

questions.question: Show an alert that says Hello.
questions.explanation: The alert() function displays a pop-up message. Put your text inside parentheses wrapped in quotation marks.

code_example.explanation: Use alert followed by parentheses and quotation marks with the word Hello inside.

lessons.title: Headings and Paragraphs
lessons.body: HTML uses tags to organize text content.
lessons.avatar_script: Headings show titles and paragraphs show text.

questions.question: Which tag creates a paragraph?
questions.explanation: The p tag creates paragraphs.

questions.question: Headings make titles on webpages.
questions.explanation: Heading tags create titles.

questions.question: Create a paragraph that says Learning Web Design.
questions.explanation: Use the p tag to create paragraphs. Write an opening p tag, your text content, then a closing p tag.

code_example.explanation: Write p tags with the sentence inside.

lessons.title: Styling Text with CSS
lessons.body: CSS can change text color and size.
lessons.avatar_script: You can style headings and paragraphs using CSS rules.

questions.question: Which property changes text size?
questions.explanation: font-size controls text size.

questions.question: CSS can style HTML headings.
questions.explanation: CSS can style any HTML element.

questions.question: Make a heading blue.
questions.explanation: Write a CSS rule targeting the h1 element and set the color property to blue inside curly braces.

code_example.explanation: Use the color property in a CSS rule targeting h1.

lessons.title: Buttons and Click Events
lessons.body: JavaScript can respond when users click buttons.
lessons.avatar_script: Buttons let users interact with webpages.

questions.question: What happens when a button is clicked in this example?
questions.explanation: The code triggers an alert.

questions.question: JavaScript can detect button clicks.
questions.explanation: JavaScript listens for events.

questions.question: Create an alert that says You clicked.
questions.explanation: Call the alert() function with your message text inside parentheses, wrapped in quotation marks.

code_example.explanation: Use the alert function with the message inside parentheses.

lessons.title: Putting Everything Together
lessons.body: HTML builds the page, CSS styles it, and JavaScript makes it interactive.
lessons.avatar_script: Now you can combine all three technologies to build a complete webpage.

code_example.code: My Page

questions.question: JavaScript can change text on a webpage.
questions.explanation: JavaScript can update HTML content.

questions.question: Which language makes webpages interactive?
questions.explanation: JavaScript adds interactivity.

questions.question: Write JavaScript that changes text to Hello World.
questions.explanation: Use getElementById() to find the element, then set its innerText property to the new text value.

code_example.explanation: Use innerText to change the content of an element.
